channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Microsoft faces challenges meeting clean energy targets amid rapid AI data center growth
item.description: Microsoft is facing an internal dilemma as its rapid expansion of AI data centers challenges its ambitious clean energy goals. The company has publicly committed to matching 100% of its hourly energy ...

item.title: Rabid beaver attack in New Jersey leads to multiple exposures and health warnings
item.description: Health officials in Mahwah, New Jersey, have confirmed that a beaver involved in multiple human encounters at Lake Henry tested positive for rabies after an attack reported on May 3. According to the ...

item.title: Hasan Piker Critiques AI and Shares His Daily Streaming Routine
item.description: Hasan Piker, a prominent left-wing Twitch streamer and political commentator, spends the majority of his days streaming and engaging with his audience on topics ranging from U.S. politics to internati...

item.title: Over 500 Terror Suspects Set for Trial in Borno After Large-Scale Investigation
item.description: Authorities for Giwa Barracks Joint Investigation Centre for Maiduguri don complete investigation of about 1,450 suspected terrorists wey security forces arrest during counter-insurgency operations. A...

item.title: Two UK Residents Self-Isolating Following Hantavirus Cases Linked to Cruise Ship
item.description: Two individuals who recently returned to the United Kingdom from the MV Hondius cruise ship have been instructed to self-isolate following a hantavirus outbreak on board, the UK Health Security Agency...

item.title: Most ASEAN Leaders Expected at 2026 Summit, Myanmar Likely Absent
item.description: The upcoming ASEAN Leaders' Summit in 2026 is set to see the attendance of heads of state from all member countries, with the notable exception of Myanmar. Officials from the ASEAN Secretariat have co...

item.title: Authorities advise parents to reinforce hand hygiene after hepatitis A cases in South Wales
item.description: Following a recent outbreak of hepatitis A in South Wales, health authorities have advised parents to pay close attention to their children's handwashing habits. The outbreak has prompted concern due ...

item.title: Exceptionally Preserved 240-Million-Year-Old Amphibian Found in Australian Garden Wall
item.description: A fossil hidden in a garden retaining wall for decades has been identified as Arenaerpeton supinatus, a 240-million-year-old amphibian. Unearthed in the 1990s by a retired chicken farmer who used rock...

item.title: Barbie Imperial recalls experiences with her “Blood vs Duty” co-stars
item.description: Barbie Imperial recently opened up about her experiences working on the TV series “Blood vs Duty,” sharing fond memories with her co-stars. In interviews, she expressed gratitude for the camaraderie a...

item.title: Focusing Your SEO Strategy on Revenue Instead of Just Rankings
item.description: Many businesses make the mistake of prioritising search engine rankings over actual revenue. While achieving a high position on Google can feel like a win, it doesn’t necessarily translate into sales ...

item.title: A Son Reflects on His Mother's Choice of MAID for a Peaceful Passing
item.description: David Sheffield shares a deeply personal account of his mother's decision to end her life through Medical Assistance in Dying (MAID) after reaching her late 90s. She had carefully planned her final ye...

item.title: Zendaya reflects on a busy year of work and family life while promoting upcoming films
item.description: Zendaya, the acclaimed actress from Oakland, California, has described 2026 as one of the most intense years of her career. Speaking to Vogue Brazil, she detailed her return to the hit series Euphoria...

item.title: HYPREP Highlights Safety and Mental Wellbeing Measures for Ogoni Cleanup Workers
item.description: The Hydrocarbon Pollution Remediation Project (HYPREP) don reassert say dem dey committed to di safety and psychosocial wellbeing of workers wey dey involved for di Ogoni cleanup. As di world dey obse...

item.title: DA Criticises Ramaphosa's Visit to Mnangagwa Amid Zimbabwe Human Rights Concerns
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) in South Africa has sharply criticised President Cyril Ramaphosa's recent visit to Zimbabwean President Emmerson Mnangagwa's private farm in Kwekwe. The DA, which is part ...

item.title: Authorities monitor passengers after Hantavirus death on Johannesburg flight, risk remains low
item.description: Global and South African health authorities are conducting contact tracing after a Dutch woman carrying Hantavirus fell seriously ill during an Airlink flight from St Helena to Johannesburg on 25 Apri...

item.title: The Role of the Global South in Shaping the Changing World Order
item.description: The global order is currently undergoing significant shifts, with increasing attention on the role of the Global South. While the West has traditionally dominated international relations, recent event...

item.title: Hantavirus cases prompt monitoring of airport staff and passengers in South Africa
item.description: Health authorities in South Africa have initiated extensive monitoring of individuals exposed to hantavirus after a woman collapsed at OR Tambo International Airport and a British national was hospita...

item.title: Potential Budget Measures Target Family Trusts to Reduce Tax Inequality
item.description: Australia's upcoming budget is drawing attention for potential reforms aimed at addressing disparities in the tax system. A key focus is the capital gains tax (CGT), with strong rumours suggesting the...

item.title: Delta women protest prolonged electricity outage, demand politicians delay campaigns until power returns
item.description: Women and youths for Delta State don dey protest serious wahala wey dey follow long-term blackout for their areas. Dem talk say politicians no suppose dey campaign for their communities until dem fix ...

item.title: Majority of Filipinos Express Trust in PNP, OCTA Survey Shows
item.description: A recent survey conducted by OCTA Research from March 19 to 25, 2026, found that 63 percent of Filipinos either strongly or somewhat trust the Philippine National Police (PNP). The survey also reveale...
